channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Air traffic controllers to get more support after a fight and latest near miss at Washington airport
item.description: Air traffic controllers at Washington's Ronald Reagan National Airport will be offered crisis counseling and additional supervision after a fight in the tower and another alarming near miss two months after a midair collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Forecasters warn of deadly floods and strong tornadoes in parts of the Midwest and South
item.description: Potentially deadly flash flooding, high-magnitude tornadoes and baseball-sized hail could hit parts of the Midwest and South on Wednesday as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged, forecasters warned.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump gives GOP senators his support as they charge ahead on tax package and spending cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican senators unveiled a budget proposal Wednesday that's central to President Donald Trump's domestic agenda of tax breaks, spending cuts and border security, but they're delaying some of the most difficult decisions, including how to pay for the multitrillion-dollar package
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: This is what forecasters mean when they talk about a 100-year flood
item.description: Weather forecasters sometimes warn of storms that unleash such unusual rain they are described as 100-year or even 500-year floods. Here’s what to know about how scientists determine how extreme a flood is and how common these extreme events are becoming.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court sides with the FDA in its dispute over sweet-flavored vaping products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday ruled for the Food and Drug Administration in its crackdown on sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen electronic cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada girl, 7, killed in Lake Tahoe ski resort boulder accident: police
item.description: A 7-year-old girl died after being struck by a falling boulder at a ski resort near Lake Tahoe in Nevada, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Naval Academy removes hundreds of library books in DEI purge
item.description: Officials for the Annapolis, Md., school were instructed to review the library late last week, though it is unclear if the directive came straight from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at DOGE
item.description: President Trump has said that DOGE will end at some point and Musk will return to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Diddy civil suit thrown out after accuser refuses to identify herself
item.description: An accuser's lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs was thrown out this week after "Jane Doe" refused to disclose her identity.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KRON4

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at Trump's DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk and President Donald Trump have recently suggested that the billionaire entrepreneur's time in the administration might be running out.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court rules in favor of FDA in dispute over flavored vapes
item.description: A unanimous Supreme Court overturned a lower court ruling and found the FDA acted lawfully when it blocked two vaping companies from marketing fruity and dessert-flavored liquids for their electronic nicotine products.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Mass layoffs rattle Head Start leaders already on edge over funding problems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The problems for Head Start began days after President Donald Trump took office. Trump's administration announced it would freeze federal grants — the primary funding for the early education program that serves more than half a million low-income children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 launches in June with new 'Mario Kart World' game
item.description: Nintendo has announced a June 5 launch date for its latest gaming console, the Switch 2, which will introduce interactive chat and screenshare functions to connect gamers.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 key takeaways as Trump’s sweeping HHS layoffs begin
item.description: Thousands of employees across the vast Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) began receiving layoff notices as early as 5 a.m. on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Tesla owner fights back with warning to Cybertruck vandal, vows to 'make an example' of suspect
item.description: A California man is blasting anti-Elon Musk protesters after his Tesla Cybertruck was vandalized in his driveway in the latest string of attacks on the brand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dirt bikes, ATVs driven around World War II memorial in Washington, DC: video
item.description: Authorities are working to identify people seen on video driving ATVs and dirt bikes inside the World War II memorial in Washington, D.C., over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Val Kilmer dies at 65
item.description: Val Kilmer, a veteran actor known for his roles as Jim Morrison and Batman, has died on Tuesday, his family confirmed to the New York Times. He was 65. His daughter, Mercedes Kilmer, said her father died of pneumonia and was surrounded by family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: US judge orders Trump administration to restore legal aid to unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in California on Tuesday ordered the Trump administration to temporarily restore legal aid to tens of thousands of migrant children who are in the United States without a parent or guardian.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. soldier from Joliet dies during training exercise in Lithuania
item.description: One of the four U.S. soldiers who died in a training exercise in Lithuania last week was from Joliet.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, WGN 9

item.title: 6 people are injured after a truck crashed into a building and pedestrians in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A box truck crashed into a building Tuesday afternoon in Boston’s Chinatown neighborhood and hit several pedestrians before flipping onto its side, city officials said. The driver and five others were injured in what authorities said appears to have been an accident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration admits it wrongly deported man to prison in El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration acknowledged late Monday that it had mistakenly deported a Salvadoran man protected from removal, sending him to a facility in El Salvador where they argue they are unable to secure his return.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: Most immigrants at risk of deportation from US are Christian, report finds
item.description: As many as four in five immigrants at risk of deportation from the United States are Christian, according to a new report that calls on their fellow believers to consider the impact of aggressive deportation policies pursued under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Peter Smith, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An ‘administrative error’ sent a Maryland man to an El Salvador prison, ICE says
item.description: President Donald Trump’ s administration has acknowledged mistakenly deporting a Maryland man with protected legal status to a notorious El Salvador prison last month, but is arguing against returning him to the United States because of his alleged gang ties and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Read is accused of striking her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O’Keefe, with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm outside of a house party.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WGN 9

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: “Tragically, three soldiers were found deceased in Lithuania yesterday, and it pains me to confirm that today the fourth soldier was also found deceased,” White House said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read's retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Jury selection began Tuesday in the retrial of Karen Read, less than a year after a judge declared a mistrial on charges that she was responsible for the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cornell student protester facing deportation leaves the US on his 'own terms' after losing faith
item.description: A Cornell University student facing deportation after his visa was revoked because of his campus activism said he decided to leave the United States. Momodou Taal, a citizen of the United Kingdom and Gambia, had asked a federal court to halt his detention.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: April Fools' Day is celebrated with pranks and hoaxes worldwide
item.description: From France to Iceland to the United States, April Fools’ Day will be celebrated on Tuesday with practical jokes and elaborate hoaxes, so make sure to triple check viral posts and don’t leave your back open to any stray sticky notes.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Aggressive sea lion attacks 15-year-old girl in Southern California waters
item.description: A 15-year-old in Long Beach, California, is recovering after a terrifying and unexpected ocean encounter with a sea lion that attacked her over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, ABC 4

item.title: Investigation into death of former Yankees player's son turns to carbon monoxide: reports
item.description: Investigators in Costa Rica say carbon monoxide may instead be to blame for the death of Miller Gardner, the 14-year-old son of former Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: A court is allowing the release of most records in the Gene Hackman death investigation
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A court on Monday cleared the way for the release of investigative records from the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, as long as depictions of the deceased couple are blocked from view.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Remains found in California decades ago ID'd as missing New York 13-year-old
item.description: A 13-year-old girl who was found dead along a river in California's Santa Cruz County three decades ago was recently identified through DNA testing.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, ABC 4
